task: R6-D — Healthcare practice state privacy add-on (Michigan medical records access + breach)
disclaimer: Operational compliance research — not legal advice. Michigan Identity Theft Protection Act deems HIPAA-compliant entities in compliance for breach notice.
policy: Public statute sources only; gaps explicit.

cited_facts.text: 'Medical record' means information in any form or medium pertaining to a patient's health care, medical history, diagnosis, prognosis, or condition maintained by a health care provider or facility in the course of caring for the patient.

A patient or authorized representative may submit a written request (signed, dated within 60 days) to examine or obtain a copy; the provider or facility must respond within 30 days (60 days if records off-site).
A health care provider or facility that receives a request for a medical record may not inquire as to the purpose for which the medical record is requested.

data_security_breach.label: MCL 445.72 — Identity Theft Protection Act (breach notice)

cited_facts.text: Unless the breach is determined not likely to cause substantial loss, injury, or identity theft, an owner or licensee of database data must notify each affected Michigan resident whose unencrypted personal information was accessed and acquired by an unauthorized person.
Notice must be provided without unreasonable delay, subject to scope-determination and law-enforcement delay exceptions.
A person or agency that complies with HIPAA (45 CFR parts 160 and 164) for unauthorized-access prevention and customer notice is considered in compliance with MCL 445.72.
